title: WorkflowConfig
description: Full state machine for a tenant/workflow type. Entirely config-driven — no states or transitions are hardcoded in the platform. Transitions support user-driven branching: multiple named transitions from the same fromState are rendered as distinct buttons in detail-view-config, each with independent roles, maker-checker rules, validation, and actions. Priority scoring uses a bounded group-scoped model with built-in guardrails against inflation and stacking.

initialState.description: State name assigned to a work item on ingestion. Must match a state declared in the states array.

name.description: Unique state name within this workflow (e.g. OPEN, UNDER_REVIEW, PENDING_SSI_SETUP, CLOSED, ESCALATED).

terminal.description: Terminal states cannot be transitioned from. SLA clocks stop. WorkItemDependency fan-out fires for dependents.

allowedRoles.description: Roles that can view work items in this state. Absent means all authenticated roles.

transitions.description: Available transitions. Multiple transitions can share the same fromState — each is rendered as a distinct button in detail-view-config, supporting user-driven branching (e.g. close-as-resolved vs forward-to-compliance from UNDER_REVIEW). Each path carries independent roles, maker-checker, validation, and actions.

name.description: Unique transition name within this workflow. Referenced by detail-view-config.actions and SLA escalationActions.

fromState.description: State the work item must be in for this transition to be available. Must match a state in the states array.

toState.description: State the work item moves to after this transition. Must match a state in the states array.

trigger.description: USER_ACTION: user explicitly fires from detail view. SYSTEM_EVENT: fires automatically when a named system event occurs. SLA_BREACH: fires automatically when escalateAfterMinutes is reached.

systemEventType.description: Required when trigger=SYSTEM_EVENT. The event name this transition listens for (e.g. CHILD_WORKFLOW_COMPLETED, KAFKA_EVENT_RECEIVED).

allowedRoles.description: Roles that can trigger this transition. Applies to USER_ACTION only. Absent means all authenticated roles.

requiresMakerChecker.description: If true, the transition requires a second approver (checker) whose userId must differ from the maker's userId. Sets WorkItem.pendingCheckerId and WorkItem.pendingCheckerTransition.

validationRules.description: Field-level preconditions that must pass before the transition is allowed. A failed validation is surfaced to the user before execution.

value.description: Comparand value. Not required for EXISTS.

errorMessage.description: Message shown to the user when this validation fails.

actions.description: Actions executed in order when this transition fires. On failure, behaviour is governed by onFailure.

onFailure.description: ROLLBACK_TRANSITION: return item to fromState and write AuditEntry(TRANSITION_FAILED). CONTINUE: log the failure and continue to next action. RETRY: retry up to maxAttempts times with backoffSeconds delay.

backoffSeconds.description: For onFailure=RETRY only. Seconds between retry attempts.

targetGroup.description: REASSIGN_GROUP: the group id to assign. Changes WorkItem.assignedGroup without requiring a separate state change. Produces AuditEntry(GROUP_REASSIGNMENT). Can be combined with a state change in the same transition.

workflowType.description: Workflow type of the child work item to create.

initialGroup.description: Group the child work item is initially assigned to.

fieldMappings.description: Field values to copy from the parent work item to the child's fields.

sourceField.description: Dot-notation path in the parent WorkItem.fields.

targetField.description: Dot-notation path in the child WorkItem.fields.

deduplicationKeyField.description: Dot-notation path in the parent WorkItem.fields whose value is used as the deduplication key. If an existing non-terminal child work item of the target workflowType has a WorkItemDependency with the same deduplicationKey value, the parent attaches to that existing child (no new work item created). Produces AuditEntry(ATTACHED_TO_EXISTING_CHILD) vs AuditEntry(CHILD_WORKFLOW_CREATED).

priorityConfig.description: Bounded, group-scoped priority scoring engine. Between groups: scores are additive. Within a group: only the highest-matching component counts (mutual exclusivity prevents stacking). maxTotalScore prevents inflation as groups accumulate. The simulatePriority Gradle task must be run before deploying priority config changes.

maxTotalScore.description: Hard ceiling on total score. validateConfigs rejects configs where sum of all groups' max possible scores exceeds maxTotalScore * 1.2. All priorityLevel.minScore values must be <= maxTotalScore.

recalculationIntervalMinutes.description: How often the background recalculation job re-scores all non-terminal work items of this type. Required because TEMPORAL_PROXIMITY scores change as time passes.

saturationAlertThreshold.description: Percentage threshold (0-100). The simulatePriority Gradle task fails the build if the projected CRITICAL% exceeds this. Default 30 — prevents CRITICAL from becoming meaningless through saturation.

componentGroups.description: Scoring component groups. Scores are additive between groups. Within each group, only the highest-matching component counts.

rationale.description: REQUIRED. Business justification for this scoring dimension. Enforced by schema to prevent undocumented components accumulating and invisibly inflating scores.

owner.description: REQUIRED. Responsible business contact (email or team name). Required for change management alerting when this group's config changes.

effectiveFrom.description: Optional. If set, this group only scores items from this date. validateConfigs warns on expired groups.

effectiveTo.description: Optional. Expired groups auto-deactivate. validateConfigs warns on groups past their effectiveTo.

components.description: Ordered array of scoring components within this group. Evaluated in order; first match wins and remaining components are skipped.

oneOf.title: TemporalProximityComponent
oneOf.description: Scores based on a date field's proximity to today (in tenant timezone). Bands must be exhaustive — a BEYOND catch-all is required. validateConfigs rejects overlapping bands and missing BEYOND.

field.description: Dot-notation path to a DATE field in WorkItem.fields.

condition.description: OVERDUE: date is in the past. TODAY: date is today. WITHIN_N_DAYS: date is within the next N days (requires days field). BEYOND: catch-all for further dates — required.

oneOf.title: NumericThresholdComponent
oneOf.description: Scores based on a MONETARY or DECIMAL field value against thresholds. Thresholds must be in descending order. A default catch-all is required. validateConfigs enforces both constraints.

field.description: Dot-notation path to a MONETARY or DECIMAL field. For MONETARY, path should reference the amount sub-field (e.g. trade.notionalAmount.amount).

operator.description: Comparison operator. Absent on default catch-all rows.

value.description: Threshold value as string for BigDecimal precision (e.g. 1000000.00). Absent on default catch-all rows.

default.description: True on the catch-all threshold — matched when no other threshold matches. Required.

oneOf.title: ConditionMatchComponent
oneOf.description: Scores points if a condition tree matches, 0 if it does not. Uses the same LEAF/GROUP condition tree syntax as routing-config.rules.conditions.

priorityLevels.description: Maps score ranges to priority level labels. Must cover the full 0 to maxTotalScore range with no gaps. validateConfigs enforces this. Levels should be declared in descending minScore order.

minScore.description: Minimum score (inclusive) for this level. The level applies until the next level's minScore.

colour.description: Hex colour for blotter row highlighting (e.g. #FF0000).

slaRules.description: SLA rules applied across states. Declared at workflow level (not per-state) to allow shared SLA policies. The SLA engine runs a scheduled job to scan non-terminal work items — scheduling is infrastructure, but SLA evaluation rules are a domain concern in platform-domain.

appliesTo.description: State names this rule applies to. Use ["*"] for all non-terminal states.

clockStartEvent.description: Determines what event starts the SLA clock. ITEM_CREATED: from ingestion timestamp. ENTERED_CURRENT_STATE: from the last state transition. ASSIGNED_TO_CURRENT_GROUP: from the last group assignment. Minutes are business-hours minutes in the tenant's timezone (weekends excluded).

warnAfterMinutes.description: Business minutes before a warning is raised. Produces AuditEntry(SLA_WARNING) and increments sla.warnings.total metric.

escalateAfterMinutes.description: Business minutes before escalation fires. Must be greater than warnAfterMinutes. Produces AuditEntry(SLA_BREACH) and increments sla.breaches.total metric.

targetGroup.description: REASSIGN_GROUP: group to reassign the item to.

transition.description: TRIGGER_TRANSITION: name of the transition to fire. Must have trigger=SLA_BREACH.

roles.description: NOTIFY_ROLES: roles to send notifications to.
